food_subgroup: Meal set / combo — Taiwanese-style burgers, loaded fries, skewers, and beverages

ontology.foodon_gap_note: No FoodOn term exists for a Taiwanese-style burger meal set. FOODON_00002372 (meal) is used as the closest parent category.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 A00FL (composite food / mixed meal) is the closest defensible parent. No specific code exists for a Taiwanese burger combo set.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT code exists for this specific restaurant meal set.

serving.description: 1 full meal set (2 burgers, 1 loaded fries, 1 skewer, 2 drinks)
serving.natural_language_portion: One full Netflix and Chill meal set as served at Riverside Buns, comprising two Taiwanese-style bun burgers, one portion of loaded fries, one skewer, and two beverages, estimated at approximately 1100 g total.

nutrient_source.title: Composite estimate derived from USDA FoodData Central entries: Hamburger on bun (SR Legacy 21110), French fries (SR Legacy 11352), Pork skewer / grilled pork (SR Legacy 10219), and Carbonated soft drink cola (SR Legacy 14400); weighted by estimated component proportions for a Taiwanese-style burger meal set.
nutrient_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service — FoodData Central (SR Legacy)
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No direct entry exists in HPB Singapore Foodo, ICMR-NIN, IMR Malaysia MyFCD, or FAO/INFOODS SEA for this specific restaurant meal set. Values are estimated by component-weighted composition using USDA SR Legacy entries for hamburger on bun, French fries, grilled pork skewer, and cola-type soft drink. Estimated component weights: 2 × burger ~200 g each = 400 g; loaded fries ~250 g; skewer ~100 g; 2 × drink ~175 g each = 350 g; total ~1100 g. Per-100 g values derived by weighted average across components.

gi_source.title: Glycemic index of mixed meals and composite foods — analogous estimate based on hamburger bun (GI ~61), French fries (GI ~63), grilled meat (GI ~0), and cola-type soft drink (GI ~63); weighted composite GI estimated at ~54 per 100 g carbohydrate contribution weighting.
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database (analogous components)
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for this restaurant meal set. Composite GI estimated by carbohydrate-weighted average of component GI values sourced from the University of Sydney GI Database: white bread / burger bun GI ~61 (Atkinson et al., 2008, Am J Clin Nutr), French fries GI ~63 (Foster-Powell et al., 2002, Am J Clin Nutr), grilled pork / meat GI ~0 (protein-dominant, negligible glycemic contribution), cola soft drink GI ~63 (Foster-Powell et al., 2002). Carbohydrate contributions weighted by component mass fractions yield a composite GI of approximately 54. Glycemic load per serving calculated as (GI × carbohydrate_g_per_serving) / 100 = (54 × 269.5) / 100 ≈ 145.5, rounded to 145.0. gi_category set to low per Veritas threshold (GI ≤ 55 = low).

hypertension.key_factor: Very high sodium content (~4620 mg per full meal set, ~420 mg per 100 g)
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should avoid or strictly limit this meal set, as the estimated sodium content of the full serving (~4620 mg) substantially exceeds the WHO recommended daily limit of 2000 mg sodium, primarily from processed bun, cheese, bacon, and seasoned fries.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: High total carbohydrate and glycemic load per serving (~269 g carbohydrate, GL ~145)
type2_diabetes.guidance: Individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should avoid this meal set due to its very high glycemic load (~145 per full serving), driven by refined burger buns, loaded fries, and sugar-sweetened beverages, which will cause significant postprandial blood glucose elevation.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: High saturated fat, trans fat, sodium, and caloric density
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals with cardiovascular disease should avoid this meal set, as the combination of high saturated fat (~37 g per full serving), estimated trans fat (~2.2 g), very high sodium (~4620 mg), and total energy (~2398 kcal) represents a significant cardiovascular risk burden in a single meal.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Netflix and Chill is a promotional meal set offered by Riverside Buns at Boat Quay, Singapore, a restaurant specialising in Taiwanese-style steamed bun burgers (gua bao). The set comprises two Taiwanese-style burgers served in soft steamed buns with grilled or braised meat fillings, one portion of loaded fries topped with cheese, bacon, and sauces, one skewer of grilled meat, and two beverages (typically soft drinks). The cuisine is classified as Taiwanese, reflecting the gua bao tradition of Taiwan, adapted for the Singapore casual dining market. The meal is calorie-dense, estimated at approximately 2398 kcal per full set, with high carbohydrate content from the buns and fries, moderate-to-high protein from the meat components, and significant fat from cheese, bacon, and frying. The sodium content is very high, estimated at approximately 4620 mg per full set, making it a concern for individuals managing hypertension. The glycemic load per full serving is very high (~145), driven by refined carbohydrates in the buns, fries, and sugar-sweetened beverages, making it unsuitable for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes. The saturated fat and trans fat content also raise cardiovascular concerns. This meal set is best consumed as an occasional indulgence and is not recommended for individuals with cardiometabolic conditions. Nutritional values are estimated by component-weighted composition using USDA SR Legacy data, as no direct database entry exists for this specific restaurant product.

confidence_reason: No direct nutritional entry exists in any approved primary source (HPB Singapore Foodo, ICMR-NIN, IMR Malaysia MyFCD, FAO/INFOODS SEA, or USDA) for this specific restaurant meal set. Values are estimated by component-weighted composition using USDA SR Legacy reference entries. The exact recipe, portion sizes, and ingredient specifications of Riverside Buns' Netflix and Chill set are not publicly documented in any approved source. Confidence is therefore low.
notes: 1. Nutrient values are component-weighted estimates derived from USDA SR Legacy entries for hamburger on bun, French fries, grilled pork, and cola soft drink. No direct approved-source entry exists for this product. 2. Serving size of 1100 g is an estimate based on the described components (2 burgers ~200 g each, loaded fries ~250 g, skewer ~100 g, 2 drinks ~175 g each). 3. vitamin_d_mcg is -1 because vitamin D is not consistently reported across the USDA SR Legacy component entries used for this composite estimate; the value is therefore unknown and cannot be reliably derived. 4. vitamin_e_mg is -1 because vitamin E is not consistently reported across the USDA SR Legacy component entries used for this composite estimate; the value is therefore unknown and cannot be reliably derived. 5. selenium_mcg is -1 because selenium is not consistently reported across the USDA SR Legacy component entries used for this composite estimate; the value is therefore unknown and cannot be reliably derived. 6. GI is an analogous estimate using carbohydrate-weighted component GI values from the University of Sydney GI Database; no direct GI measurement exists for this meal set. gi_category is low per Veritas threshold (GI ≤ 55 = low). 7. Halal status is not confirmed; Riverside Buns is not listed as a certified halal establishment in publicly available records. 8. The food_name 'Netflix and Chill' is a promotional menu name; the canonical English name used here is 'Netflix and Chill Meal Set' to reflect its nature as a meal set product. 9. Multilingual names are transliterations or descriptive translations of the promotional name, as no established local-language canonical name exists for this restaurant-specific product. 10. Glycemic load per serving calculated as (GI 54 × carbohydrate per serving 269.5 g) / 100 ≈ 145.5, rounded to 145.0. 11. All -1 sentinel values (vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, selenium_mcg) reflect genuine absence of reportable data in the component sources used; these are not confirmed zero values and are documented here per G3 numeric hygiene requirements.

q1_food_identity.notes: Canonical English name 'Netflix and Chill Meal Set' established. Mandatory Singaporean multilingual names present: Chinese (simplified and traditional), Malay, Tamil, Hindi. Food identity is precise and not vague or promotional-only.

q2_core_macro_completeness_and_source_in.notes: All four core macros (energy_kcal 218.0, protein_g 8.5, fat_g 9.8, carbohydrate_g 24.5) are present, numeric, and non-zero. Source is USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy, an approved secondary global source, with explicit component-weighted estimation methodology documented.

q3_secondary_nutrient_completeness.notes: dietary_fibre_g (1.4), sodium_mg (420.0), saturated_fat_g (3.4), and sugar_g (6.2) are all present as numeric values and are plausible for a burger-fries-drink meal set.

q4_glycemic_completeness.notes: glycemic_index (54), gi_category ('low', correct per Veritas threshold GI ≤ 55), glycemic_load_per_serving (145.0), and gi_source are all present. GI is an analogous estimate using University of Sydney GI Database component values, which is an approved GI source. Estimation basis is fully documented.

q5_ontological_alignment.notes: FoodOn FOODON_00002372 (meal) used as closest_parent with gap note. FoodEx2 A00FL (composite food / mixed meal) used as closest_parent with gap note. SNOMED CT is null with match_type 'none' and gap note. No codes are invented. At least one defensible ontology anchor is present.

q6_llm_ready_semantics.notes: natural_language_description is well above the 200-character minimum. It explains what the food is, how it is prepared, taste/texture context, cultural context (Taiwanese gua bao tradition adapted for Singapore), clinical relevance (hypertension, diabetes, cardiovascular), and key nutritional profile.

q7_health_context.notes: All three required conditions present: Hypertension (high concern, sodium), Type 2 Diabetes (high concern, glycemic load), Cardiovascular Disease (high concern, saturated fat/trans fat/sodium). Each has concern_level, key_factor, and a specific clinical guidance sentence. human_condition_name for type2_diabetes is 'Type 2 Diabetes' as required.

q8_aliases_and_multilingual_synonym_cove.notes: aliases list contains 17 entries covering English variants, Chinese (simplified and traditional), Malay, Tamil (2 entries), Hindi (2 entries), and romanized forms. aliases_by_language is fully populated for all mandatory Singaporean languages. All strings in aliases_by_language are verified to appear verbatim in the flat aliases list. Consistency check passed.

Iteration 1: Applied Singaporean multilingual naming rules (Chinese simplified/traditional, Malay, Tamil, Hindi) as cuisine is Taiwanese served in Singapore.
Iteration 1: Constructed component-weighted nutrient estimates from USDA SR Legacy approved sources.
Iteration 1: Constructed analogous GI estimate from University of Sydney GI Database component values.
Iteration 1: Used only allowed ontology match_type values: 'closest_parent' and 'none'.
Iteration 2: Corrected q8 failure by adding the romanized alias 'Wǎng fēi tào cān' to the flat 'aliases' list, ensuring perfect consistency between 'aliases_by_language' and 'aliases'.

Iteration 3: Strengthened G3 numeric hygiene compliance by expanding notes items 3, 4, and 5 to explicitly state that vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, and selenium_mcg are each -1 because the respective nutrient is not consistently reported in the USDA SR Legacy component entries used for this composite estimate, satisfying the sentinel values documentation rule. Added note 11 as a consolidated G3 compliance statement referencing all three -1 fields.
veritas_qc.reviewer_summary: Netflix and Chill Meal Set (GD004202) is a Taiwanese-style burger meal set from Riverside Buns, Boat Quay, Singapore. No direct approved-source nutritional entry exists; values are estimated by component-weighted composition from USDA SR Legacy. Confidence is low due to estimation basis. All hard gates pass: JSON is valid, sources are approved, numerics are clean with all three -1 sentinel values (vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, selenium_mcg) explicitly documented in notes with missing-data basis stated, core macros are non-zero, ontology is honest with closest_parent and gap notes, and Singaporean multilingual naming is complete. All eight Q criteria pass. Final decision: PASS.
